preamble: The shift from the high back rounded vowel [u] (often in the context of a [ju] diphthong) to the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] (represented here as 'sh') is a prominent form of palatalization and coalescent assimilation, particularly in English.

examples.note: Historical palatalization where the tongue moved back toward the palate for the [j] glide, eventually merging the [s] and glide into [ʃ].
Assimilation of the alveolar fricative and the following palatal segment into a postalveolar fricative.
Historical and synchronic palatalization processes where high vowels trigger shifts in the place of articulation toward the palate.
